statement.latex: (J.\,W.\,Grossman). The nilpotent-completion diagram\/ of a group$G$is as follows:$G/ _1G G/ _2G$, where$_iG$is the$i$th term of the lower central series and the arrows are natural homomorphisms. It is easy to see that every nilpotent-completion diagram$G_1 G_2$is a$$-dia\-gram, that is, every sequence$1 _sG_{s+1} G_{s+1} G_s 1$, \$s=1,\,2,$, is exact.
